data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead in an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the contract: every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs.
- Description names multiple feature-sized pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, use a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, children once more; reject third-level splits with an explanation.

If too big, decompose:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add` only for legitimate child-to-child or sibling/replacement edges; never the parent.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
4. Write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path with child IDs, then stop.

Decomposition alone is success. Don't mix it with implementation.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC to concrete evidence (test, file, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Run git/index mutations sequentially; don't parallelize `git add`, `git commit`, or staging/commit commands.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- If `lefthook run pre-commit` depends on staged files, rerun it after staging the exact commit set. A `no-staged-files` run is not acceptance evidence.
- Commit exactly once when green; subject ends with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- Current-bead lifecycle is orchestrator-owned. Do not run `ddx bead update <bead-id> --claim`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --status <status>`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --unclaim`, or `ddx bead close <bead-id>`. Step 0 allows `ddx bead create`, `ddx bead dep add` for child-to-child or sibling/replacement edges, and `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
- If you cannot finish, write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path before exiting. No commit or rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`.

## no_changes contract

The `no_changes_rationale.txt` file under the bead metadata `bundle` path must contain one of:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` are rejected.

For orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Reports go under the bead metadata `bundle` path in `.ddx/executions/`. **Never write reports to `/tmp` or outside the repo**. Use a named in-repo path when given; otherwise write `<short-name>.md` there. Commit it with the code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other instruction files unless the bead asks.

## Long-running matrix/benchmark beads

For expensive commands (benchmarks, load tests, validation > 60s per variant):

- Write a matrix plan before launching expensive commands: list required configs, output paths, completion criteria.
- Do not re-run the same long-running command unless: (1) the command fingerprint changed (args/env/config), AND (2) you document why prior output is invalid and what changed.
- If a long-running command times out or is incomplete, exit with `status: open` + retryable `reason` in `no_changes_rationale.txt`. Do not silently retry; let the orchestrator decide.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-171dc478">
    <title>ddx work --watch: self-cycle between tasks — re-exec on a newer installed binary so workers never run stale code</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM: `ddx work --watch` runs the binary it was launched with for its ENTIRE lifetime. When newer code is installed (the factory merges fixes + `make install`), the worker keeps executing STALE logic until manually killed+restarted. It only WARNS ("installed ddx is built from X; source HEAD is Y (N commits ahead); run make install") — no action. Observed live: a 2-worker drain ran ~75 commits behind, missing ALREADY-LANDED concurrency fixes (ddx-33530e68 post-land-outside-lock, ddx-0b659710 atomic-claim-release, ddx-274943fc worktree-leak) and repeatedly colliding on the durable-audit commit — the very fixes were sitting in source but not in the running binary. Operators must manually cycle workers. Operator expectation: "the workers are supposed to know how to cycle automatically between tasks." (A stopgap supervisor that runs `ddx work --once` + `make install` between cycles is in place; this bead is the durable native fix.)

ROOT CAUSE: the --watch loop loads its binary once at startup; there is no between-task self-refresh / re-exec. The staleness warning (grep "installed ddx is built from" / "source HEAD") is informational only.

PROPOSED FIX: At a SAFE boundary in the --watch loop — after a bead completes/unclaims and BEFORE claiming the next — detect whether the on-disk installed binary differs from the running process's build commit (compare the embedded build commit vs the installed binary's reported commit, or hash/stat the executable resolved via os.Executable/PATH). If newer, re-exec the process in place (syscall.Exec) on the fresh binary, preserving argv/flags (--watch, --min-power, etc.) so the next bead runs on the latest code. NEVER re-exec mid-bead. Gate behind --self-refresh (default ON for --watch). This delivers "workers cycle automatically between tasks."

NON-SCOPE: triggering `make install`/the build from the worker (separate concern — a supervisor/build pipeline produces the binary; this bead re-execs onto it); the durable-audit collision classifier (ddx-83361480/e8bf0695/2965925e).
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. TestWorkWatch_ReexecsOnNewerInstalledBinaryBetweenBeads: with --watch, after a bead completes, if the installed binary commit differs from the running build commit, the worker re-execs (via an injectable re-exec seam) preserving argv; the next bead runs on the new binary.
2. TestWorkWatch_DoesNotReexecMidBead: re-exec happens only at the between-bead boundary, never while a bead executes.
3. TestWorkWatch_NoReexecWhenBinaryUnchanged: no re-exec when the installed binary matches the running build commit.
4. A --self-refresh flag (default on for --watch) gates the behavior; --self-refresh=false disables it.
5. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... -run 'TestWorkWatch_.*Reexec|SelfRefresh' -count=1 green.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>area:agent, area:workers, kind:bug, kind:reliability, phase:iterate, spec:FEAT-010, incident:stale-binary-collision</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-3106674f" base-rev="f0a9acecf4adb022b0acb60b33b4dd6b955ef44c" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260607T045327-f079083c"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. The bead description above is the primary contract. If it names files, specs, or prior beads, read them first. Ground decisions in repository state; do not guess.</note>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
Implemented the watch-mode self-refresh/re-exec path and committed it.

What changed:
- Added `--self-refresh` to `ddx work`, defaulting to on for watch mode, with `--self-refresh=false` disabling it in [`/Users/erik/Projects/.ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-171dc478-20260607T045327-f079083c/cli/cmd/work.go`](file:///Users/erik/Projects/.ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-171dc478-20260607T045327-f079083c/cli/cmd/work.go).
- Added the binary refresh probe, snapshot comparison, argv preservation, and injectable re-exec seam in [`/Users/erik/Projects/.ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-171dc478-20260607T045327-f079083c/cli/cmd/work_binary_refresh.go`](file:///Users/erik/Projects/.ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-171dc478-20260607T045327-f079083c/cli/cmd/work_binary_refresh.go).
- Added Unix and Windows re-exec implementations in [`/Users/erik/Projects/.ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-171dc478-20260607T045327-f079083c/cli/cmd/work_binary_refresh_unix.go`](file:///Users/erik/Projects/.ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-171dc478-20260607T045327-f079083c/cli/cmd/work_binary_refresh_unix.go) and [`/Users/erik/Projects/.ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-171dc478-20260607T045327-f079083c/cli/cmd/work_binary_refresh_windows.go`](file:///Users/erik/Projects/.ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-171dc478-20260607T045327-f079083c/cli/cmd/work_binary_refresh_windows.go).
- Wired the refresh check into the watch loop boundary and added the mid-bead guard test in [`/Users/erik/Projects/.ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-171dc478-20260607T045327-f079083c/cli/cmd/execute_loop_shared.go`](file:///Users/erik/Projects/.ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-171dc478-20260607T045327-f079083c/cli/cmd/execute_loop_shared.go) and [`/Users/erik/Projects/.ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-171dc478-20260607T045327-f079083c/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_stay_alive_test.go`](file:///Users/erik/Projects/.ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-171dc478-20260607T045327-f079083c/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_stay_alive_test.go).
- Added focused coverage in [`/Users/erik/Projects/.ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-171dc478-20260607T045327-f079083c/cli/cmd/work_binary_refresh_test.go`](file:///Users/erik/Projects/.ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-171dc478-20260607T045327-f079083c/cli/cmd/work_binary_refresh_test.go) and the flag assertion in [`/Users/erik/Projects/.ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-171dc478-20260607T045327-f079083c/cli/cmd/work_test.go`](file:///Users/erik/Projects/.ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-171dc478-20260607T045327-f079083c/cli/cmd/work_test.go).

Verification:
- `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... -run 'TestWorkWatch_.*Reexec|SelfRefresh' -count=1`
- `lefthook run pre-commit`

Commit:
- `824a673bd` `fix: self-refresh watch workers [ddx-171dc478]`
